skill.name: emailcampaignskill
skill.description: Plan, review, QA, and analyze email campaign workflows with clear approval gates and operational safeguards.
skill.summary: Agent-safe guidance for email campaign planning, audience QA, creative review, launch readiness, and post-send analysis.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged six possible weak cryptographic algorithm matches. AI review found they are false positives from normal prose and metadata, with no cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, network behavior, commands, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert on agents/openai.yaml line 2 is ordinary short description text for the skill. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain marketplace metadata describing campaign workflows. There is no code, key material, hashing, encryption, or prompt injection text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert on references/operating-checklist.md line 27 is a risk gate mentioning destructive cleanup. It is an operational safety checklist, not cryptographic guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a list of high-risk email operations that require approval. No weak algorithm, executable code, or suspicious instruction appears on the line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert on SKILL.md line 3 is frontmatter description text for when to use the skill. It does not define or recommend cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes campaign planning and review triggers. No security algorithm, hidden instruction, encoded data, or system override is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert on SKILL.md line 20 is a boundary telling agents to describe provider steps operationally. It is unrelated to encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The likely match is inside normal wording about describing provider-specific steps. The line adds a safety boundary and contains no dangerous pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert on SKILL.md line 28 is use-case prose saying the skill is designed for reviewable email work. No crypto or execution behavior appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is descriptive documentation only. It does not contain code, external calls, secret handling, or prompt injection language.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert on SKILL.md line 44 is review guidance about deliverability and list-quality context. It is not a cryptographic recommendation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line concerns email performance analysis. There is no weak algorithm use, sensitive data flow, or instruction that changes agent security behavior.

content.value_statement: Email campaigns can fail when briefs, audience rules, creative, and approvals are disconnected. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for reviewable campaign operations.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured campaign briefs with objectives, audiences, offers, metrics, constraints, and approval owners.
Reviews audience logic for inclusions, exclusions, consent, suppression state, and expected recipient counts.
Checks creative alignment across subject lines, preview text, body copy, CTAs, landing pages, and follow-up paths.
Builds launch QA checklists for links, tracking, merge tags, rendering, plain text, footer, and replies.
Frames post-send retrospectives using baseline performance, segment data, source context, and test hypotheses.
limitations: It does not send email, import contacts, change DNS, or edit production automations.
It cannot verify consent, suppression state, or list quality without source material from the user.
It provides operational recommendations, not legal compliance approval.
It depends on the user to supply platform details, campaign assets, and performance data.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Launch Brief
use_cases.description: Turn a loose campaign idea into a reviewable brief with goals, audience rules, offer details, timing, and owners.

use_cases.title: Audit Audience Logic
use_cases.description: Check segment rules, exclusions, suppression handling, consent assumptions, and expected counts before a campaign reaches production.

use_cases.title: Run a Post-Send Review
use_cases.description: Compare results against baselines, identify segment patterns, document lessons, and define the next test or recovery action.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Campaign Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an email campaign brief. Include objective, audience, offer, send window, success metric, constraints, and approval owner.

prompt_templates.title: Review Campaign Creative
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my subject line, preview text, body copy, CTA, landing page, and follow-up path for promise match.

prompt_templates.title: Check Launch Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build a launch checklist for links, UTM parameters, merge tags, rendering, plain-text fallback, footer, and reply handling.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Campaign Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze campaign performance against baseline, segment, source, deliverability context, and the original hypothesis.

output_examples.output: A launch readiness review with brief status, audience checks, creative alignment, tracking QA, approval gates, risks, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Help me understand why this campaign underperformed.
output_examples.output: A retrospective that separates deliverability, audience quality, creative promise, landing page fit, timing, and test design.

output_examples.input: Turn this idea into an email campaign plan.
output_examples.output: A structured campaign brief with objective, target audience, offer, send timing, measurement plan, constraints, and review owner.

best_practices: Provide the brief, audience rules, creative, landing page, tracking plan, and approval requirements together.
Separate recommendations from live-system actions, especially sends, imports, DNS changes, and automation edits.
Include baseline metrics and deliverability context when asking for performance analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to approve legal, consent, or sending authority questions without human review.
Do not use incomplete audience data to infer consent, suppression status, or sensitive cohort eligibility.
Do not treat one metric as the full campaign result without segment and deliverability context.

faq.question: Can this skill send email campaigns?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning, QA, analysis, and handoff. It requires explicit human approval before any live-system action.

faq.question: Does it work with specific email platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, when you provide the platform context. It describes provider steps in operational language instead of assuming hidden platform access.

faq.question: Can it verify consent or suppression status?
faq.answer: No. It can review supplied evidence, but it does not assume missing consent, suppression, or list-quality data.

faq.question: What inputs are most useful?
faq.answer: Provide the goal, audience rules, exclusions, offer, creative, landing page, send timing, tracking plan, and approval owner.

faq.question: Can it review campaign performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares results against baselines, segments, sources, deliverability context, and the original campaign hypothesis.

faq.question: Is this only for marketing teams?
faq.answer: No. Lifecycle, operations, product, support, and analytics teams can use it for reviewable email work.
